rules.rule_text:
title: Powershell MS Defender Tampering - ScriptBlockLogging
id: 9e50b0e6-464b-4eb3-858c-690d2d51e812
related:
- id: 14c71865-6cd3-44ae-adaa-1db923fae5f2
  type: derived
status: experimental
description: Detects powershell scripts attempting to disable MS Defender components
  using Set-MpPreference as performed by Vice Society ransomware gang. This includes
  additional techniques to evade existing rules by feeding in a proxy value of $true
  using a powershell boolean expression like (0 -eq $false).
references:
- https://www.educba.com/powershell-boolean/
- https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/powershell/module/defender/set-mppreference?view=windowsserver2022-ps
- https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/security/blog/2022/10/25/dev-0832-vice-society-opportunistic-ransomware-campaigns-impacting-us-education-sector/
author: Micah Babinski
date: 2022/11/26
tags:
- attack.defense_evasion
- attack.t1562
- attack.t1562.001
logsource:
  product: windows
  category: ps_script
  definition: Script block logging must be enabled
detection:
  selection_options_disabling_preference:
    ScriptBlockText|contains|all:
    - Set-MpPreference
    - -DisableRealtimeMonitoring
  filter:
    ScriptBlockText|re: .*Set-MpPreference.*Disable.*\s(\$false|0).*
  condition: selection_options_disabling_preference and not filter
falsepositives:
- Legitimate PowerShell scripts which need to disable MS Defender.
level: high
